blueprint:
FINAL: "Budget-honesty contract with a delta-gated, bounded error ladder" â€” B's contract skeleton carrying A's de-vacuated decimation engine, with every fatal flaw from both critiques repaired.

ROOT CAUSE (verified at decimateConforming.ts:113-124): an absolute-mm value (0.01 x bbox diagonal, ~2.3mm on a default pot) is passed into meshoptimizer's RELATIVE target_error slot with only ['LockBorder'] â€” installed v1.0.1 binding confirms 'ErrorAbsolute'=4 exists and is unset â€” so the error brake reads as >100% of mesh extent and the simplifier collapses unconstrained to the budget. Every 400k sliver in authoritative-2026-06.json is this one bug plus the absence of any gate behind it (the call at ParametricExportComputer.ts:2674 has no options beyond target; validation at :2685 is explicitly "REPORTING, not gating"; feature accounting at :2633-2656 runs PRE-decimation and is blind).

ARCHITECTURE (three layers, one honesty channel):
1. PRE-TRIANGULATION CAP stays the primary, quality-preserving budget actuator (searchBudgetScale 'cap', ConformingWall.ts:248-299, untouched behavior) but becomes OBSERVABLE: it returns {scale, telemetry} (floorLeaves, chosenScale, capSaturated) threaded through WatertightAssemblyResult into the new ExportBudgetReport. This closes critique-B fatal #1: a budget met at capScale 2-3 ships up to 3x the profile sag in low-curvature regions â€” the report now carries capScale and effectiveMaxSagMm = capScale x profile sag, and all "full detail" UI wording is conditional on capScale==1.
2. RESIDUAL GAP -> quality-bounded decimation: decimateConforming is rewritten as a deterministic absolute-error ladder. Flags = ['ErrorAbsolute','LockBorder'] (LockBorder kept as inert defense only â€” provably nothing to lock on a closed pot; never relied on). Seed = the profile's epsPosMm (qMaxSag, already in scope at :2322 â€” decimation starts by spending exactly the fidelity triangulation was allowed); rungs double to a 0.2mm ceiling (~FDM layer height, dev-overridable); each rung tried plain then with 'Regularize' (the only shape lever in 1.0.1, still under the same absolute cap). EVERY attempt restarts from the ORIGINAL mesh â€” sound because plain simplify only re-indexes over the untouched vertex array, so retries are exact, cannot compound, and positions remain exact analytic surface points BY CONSTRUCTION (the hard constraint), pinned by an exact-subset unit test. Ladder inputs are guarded (seed clamped >= 1e-4 kills the e*=2 infinite loop; ceiling = max(ceiling, seed)).
3. ENFORCED PER-CANDIDATE GATE, never assumed (meshopt guarantees neither shape nor topology â€” issues #346/#477, 75-degree flip heuristic), cheap-first ordering with per-attempt elapsed ms:
   a. triangleQuality3D (cheap O(N)) â€” DELTA-gated: sliverDelta <= 0 and maxAspect3D <= max(input maxAspect, ASPECT_MAX). Delta, not absolute-zero, fixes critique-B fatal #3: a pre-defective input (Voronoi nm=2 carve-out, extreme dims) does not poison the applied branch forever nor corrupt the refusal taxonomy, and the report carries inputDefects so refusal reasons never misattribute pre-existing damage to decimation.
   b. FOLD-OVER GUARD (closes the geometric-flip hole both critiques flagged: combinatorial orientationMismatches at :381/:397 cannot see a winding-consistent geometric inversion): area-weighted vertex normals computed ONCE from the input mesh; a candidate triangle whose normal opposes the sum of its three input vertex normals is folded; gate requires foldedDelta <= 0 (delta-based, robust at sharp creases).
   c. topologyMetric (expensive, position-welded) â€” bnd/nonMan/orient deltas <= 0, using the SAME primitives + thresholds summarizeConformingValidation uses (topologyMetric + WELD_TOL_MM=1e-4 + triangleQuality3D + ASPECT_MAX=100, verified at :372-413 and fidelity/types.ts:67-69), so an accepted candidate can never subsequently fail the production validity throw at useParametricExport.ts:365-371.
   d. CALLER FEATURE GATE: validateCandidate receives the RAW PRE-COMPACTION index buffer (index-parallel to the asm.vertices (u,t,surfaceId) stash, verified :2646-2651; pos3D[j] = eval(asm.vertices[j]) per :2581-2599). The call site's closure measures feature coverage on the SURVIVING outer-wall vertex subset via measureFeatureResolution and STASHES the FeatureResolutionResult; on acceptance that stashed result becomes LAST_CONFORMING_FEATURE_RESULT, so featDrop describes the SHIPPED mesh. This fixes critique-A's fatal flaw (no phantom asmIndicesOfShipped; compacted ids never index asm.vertices) AND critique-B's fatal #2 (the rebuilt feature graph is HOISTED out of the accounting try-block; if no graph is obtainable the call site refuses decimation conservatively with reason 'feature-gate-unmeasurable' and sets LAST_CONFORMING_FEATURE_RESULT honestly â€” never a stale pre-decimation readout). Documented limit: featDrop=0 is vertex-proximity coverage, not crest-edge-chain integrity; the named escalation if that ever matters for print quality is simplifyWithAttributes vertex_lock on feature-line vertices.
4. HONEST REFUSAL â€” the exact product contract: when no ladder point passes, the export SUCCEEDS with the natural watertight mesh (never a throw, never a damaged mesh, never a silent no-op). Three synchronized channels: (a) structured ExportBudgetReport on ParametricExportResult (requested/built/delivered tris, decimation verdict 'not-needed'|'applied'|'refused', refusalReason, decimationErrorMm, capScale, capSaturated, effectiveMaxSagMm, estimatedStlMB â€” explicitly labeled binary-STL-equivalent since 3MF is the dialog default and several-fold smaller); (b) a reason-derived warning (using the ACTUAL configured ceiling, not hardcoded text, and distinct wording for wasm-unavailable/simplify-threw) appended to validationSummary.warnings, which the existing ExportDialog warning panel already renders (verified ExportDialog.tsx:566-570) â€” validationSummary.valid stays MEASURED (true on the clean natural mesh) so the hook's invalid-mesh throw is untouched; (c) the hook completion message states the actual delivered size. This replaces three verified dishonest outcomes today: slivered mesh in the harness, thrown 'Export validation failed: N sliver triangle(s)' in production (a refusal is a strict UX upgrade over that throw), and the silent over-budget no-op when WASM is missing (now reason='wasm-unavailable', surfaced).

DETERMINISM: fixed ladder, fixed flag schedule, single-threaded WASM, restart-from-original per attempt -> identical output for identical inputs, scoped to same-machine/same-lockfile (pos3D is GPU-evaluated upstream; cross-machine byte-identity is unattainable regardless). meshoptimizer is pinned EXACTLY to 1.0.1 (caret removed) and the version is recorded inside the committed baseline so a behavior-changing bump is detectable, never silent.

NO-OP SAFETY: at the production default ('high' profile, ~4M budget) the decimation branch never fires at default dims â€” gate layer 3 proves byte-identity against mesh-hashes-default-2026-06.json, so the entire change is inert on the shipping default path.

PROCESS (CLAUDE.md mandatory): impact({target:'decimateConforming'}), impact({target:'summarizeConformingValidation'}), impact({target:'searchBudgetScale'}) before editing â€” HIGH risk expected on the export path, warn the user; detect_changes({scope:'compare', base_ref:'apply/streamlit-fix'}) before commit. Callers verified at HEAD: production call site ParametricExportComputer.ts:2674, barrel re-export conforming/index.ts:71-72 (must re-export the new types), and the test file.

changes.what: Core rewrite: ErrorAbsolute+mm units fix, guarded deterministic ladder (seed=profile sag, x2 rungs, 0.2mm ceiling, plain-then-Regularize), delta-based topo/shape gate with fold-over guard, caller feature gate on raw pre-compaction indices, structured report with honest refusal. Return type changes MeshData -> DecimateConformingResult (callers: ParametricExportComputer.ts:2674, conforming/index.ts:71-72 barrel, the test file). loadSimplifier()/isConformingDecimationAvailable() unchanged; boundingBoxDiagonal() deleted (it WAS the bug). compactMesh dynamic import keeps its try/catch (never-throws contract preserved).

Telemetry-only change to searchBudgetScale (:248): return {scale, telemetry} instead of a bare number â€” same window [1, MAX_BUDGET_SCALE=4], same 5-step geometric binary search, same BUDGET_TOLERANCE; just track the last leaf count and report saturation. The single internal call site (:548) destructures .scale; ConformingWallResult (:141) gains optional budget?: WallBudgetTelemetry stored by buildConformingWall when the search ran. This makes the cap's three failure modes (MAX_BUDGET_SCALE saturation; scale-independent floors â€” minUniformLevel crease lattice, featureLevel refinement, creaseRefine, pinBoundaryLevel+balancing; leaves->tris slack) observable instead of inferred, and supplies the chosenScale the honesty report needs.

Thread per-wall telemetry up (metadata only, vertices/indices byte-identical): WatertightAssemblyResult (:261) gains optional budgetReport carrying both walls' WallBudgetTelemetry plus requested/per-wall budgets; populated after the two buildConformingWall calls.

ExportBudgetReport contract type + optional field on ParametricExportResult (:139, next to validationSummary :160). Refusal is NOT a validation failure: the delivered mesh is always clean; the report is the single structured honesty channel. builtTriangles is documented as the POST-CAP assembled count (NOT 'natural' â€” critique-B fatal #1), capScale/effectiveMaxSagMm expose the cap's fidelity spend, and the size estimate is explicitly binary-STL-equivalent (3MF default is several-fold smaller).

Call-site rewrite (:2658-2718) + LAST_CONFORMING_BUDGET_REPORT stash/getter (mirroring the LAST_CONFORMING_* pattern at :256-353) + dev levers __pfDecimateErrMm/__pfDecimateErrCeilMm in the qOv block (:2301). Four fixes: (1) hoist the rebuilt feature graph out of the accounting try-block into featureGraphForGate (fixes the featureGraph-null bypass); (2) profile-sag-seeded ladder via decimateConforming with a validateCandidate closure that measures featDrop on the SURVIVING vertex subset using RAW pre-compaction indices against the asm.vertices stash (verified index-parallel, :2581-2599/:2646-2651) and stashes the result â€” on acceptance it becomes LAST_CONFORMING_FEATURE_RESULT, so featDrop describes the shipped mesh, never a stale pre-dec readout; (3) no obtainable graph => conservative refusal 'feature-gate-unmeasurable' (decimator not invoked); (4) refusal warning derived from report.reason + ACTUAL ceiling pushed into validationSummary.warnings (renders via ExportDialog.tsx:566-570) while valid stays measured. [CONFORMING-FULL] log gains the verdict columns. MUST run impact() on decimateConforming + summarizeConformingValidation first per CLAUDE.md; HIGH risk expected â€” warn user.

Thread the report into ParametricExportStats (:383-399) and the completion message (:407-411); expose a synchronously-set lastBudgetReport ref so ExportPanel reads the verdict cause-accurately (not state in the same tick, not an inferred triangleCount>tris). The invalid-mesh throw at :365-376 is UNCHANGED â€” after this fix a low budget no longer produces an invalid mesh, so production low-budget exports stop throwing and succeed honestly oversize. Wording is conditional on capScale (never claims 'full detail' when the cap coarsened).

Minimal UI honesty at the dialog handler (:233-245): read the hook's lastBudgetReport (cause-accurate â€” never inferred from triangleCount>tris, which would misattribute legacy-path or wasm-unavailable cases) and surface the actual size before download. The structured warning already renders in the dialog warning panel for free (validationSummary.warnings -> ExportDialog.tsx:566-570); this adds only the pre-download notice. Whether it is a blocking confirm() or a passive notice is a product decision (see productDecisions).

New diagnoseBudget() on FidelityApi that generates the mesh ONCE and measures EVERYTHING on that single mesh (budget stash + topo + quality + featDrop stash) â€” one build per probe row instead of three independent generateMesh calls (fixes the same-mesh-by-assumption hole and the 3x probe cost; verified each existing diagnose* regenerates at :498/:516/:525).

The acceptance probe (Stage-0 A/B pattern, same config as _conforming_dimspace_probe.cjs: default dims {H:120, top_od:140, bottom_od:90, r_drain:10}, real WebGPU, ?fidelity, __pfConforming=true). Per style x budget: ONE diagnoseBudget call (single build). Budgets: 400,000 (probe-historical RED row), 499,998 (Draft floor, user-reachable), plus 100,000 on ArtDeco+DragonScales (deep-refusal exercise). Output committed as e2e/baselines/budget-honesty-2026-06.json WITH the meshoptimizer version recorded.
Barrel update (:71-72): re-export the new types alongside the changed function.

Pin meshoptimizer EXACTLY (determinism + baseline validity boundary): "^1.0.1" -> "1.0.1". A future 1.1.x upgrade (RegularizeLight/Vertex_Priority, altered simplifier internals) is a deliberate re-baselining event, never a silent lockfile drift.

testPlan:
TDD ORDER â€” every unit test is written RED against HEAD first (they genuinely fail today: flags are ['LockBorder'] with a bbox-scaled error, the return is a bare MeshData, and there is no gate), then the module is implemented GREEN, then the call site, then telemetry, then the probe. Fixtures stay at pot scale (the existing buildIcosphere already defaults radius=50mm) so absolute-mm thresholds exercise representative regimes.

UNIT (decimateConforming.test.ts, vitest):
1. UNITS REGRESSION PIN (mock-level, runs WITHOUT WASM â€” the permanent pin on the root cause): vi.mock('meshoptimizer') with a capturing simplify stub; assert the call receives stride=3 (FLOAT elements), target_error === the seed in mm (NOT multiplied by bbox diagonal), and flags containing BOTH 'ErrorAbsolute' and 'LockBorder'. RED on HEAD.
2. EXACT-SUBSET POSITIONS (pins 'removes but never moves', the hard constraint): every output vertex string-key (x|y|z) of an applied decimation is present in the input vertex set.
3. DE-VACUATED APPLY (a decimator that always refuses MUST fail this): icosphere(6, r=50) = 81,920 tris, target 20,000, seed 0.5mm (generous â€” sag at that density is ~0.01mm) â€” hard-assert report.applied === true AND report.reason === 'accepted', then triangleQuality3D sliverCount===0 / maxAspect3D<100, topologyMetric bnd=nonMan=orient=0, and resultErrorMm <= 0.5.
4. ANISOTROPIC SHAPE GATE (the 2026-06-11 gap): stretchZ(icosphere(6), 20) fixture (collapse-prone elongated triangles), target 4,000 â€” EITHER applied with the full clean gate (sliver=0, maxAspect<100, bnd=nonMan=orient=0, foldedDelta<=0) OR refused with out.mesh.indices === input.indices (untouched reference). Both branches legal; a slivered middle fails.
5. HONEST REFUSAL: icosphere(6), target 50, seed 0.0005, ceiling 0.001 â€” report.refused===true, reason==='budget-unreachable-within-error', original references returned.
6. LADDER INPUT GUARDS: errorAbsMm=0 terminates (clamped seed; would infinite-loop in the naive e*=2); seed > ceiling degrades to a single rung at the seed (attempts.length <= 2).
7. WASM-UNAVAILABLE REFUSAL (implemented, not a comment stub): vi.doMock('meshoptimizer', () => ({ MeshoptSimplifier: { supported: false } })) + vi.resetModules() + dynamic re-import â€” report.refused===true, reason==='wasm-unavailable' (the silent-no-op dishonesty path stays observed).
8. CALLER GATE: a validateCandidate that rejects everything forces ladder exhaustion with reason 'no-quality-safe-point' and the reject string recorded in attempts[i].externalReject; a validateCandidate that accepts is invoked with the RAW pre-compaction Uint32Array (max index < input vertexCount, buffer !== out.mesh.indices after compaction).
9. DELTA GATE ON DEFECTIVE INPUT: a fixture with one pre-existing sliver â€” an applied decimation that adds no NEW sliver is accepted (sliverDelta<=0), and report.inputDefects records the pre-existing one (refusal taxonomy can never misattribute).
10. DETERMINISM (same-process scope): two runs on identical input produce byte-identical index buffers.
11. EXISTING 3 tests updated to destructure { mesh, report } â€” assertions unchanged. Full suite: npm run test -- decimateConforming, then the whole 318-test suite green. EXPLICIT NON-EVIDENCE NOTE (in the test file header AND the gate doc): WASM-less unit green is NOT fix evidence â€” tests 2-5/8-10 self-skip or run mocked there; only the WebGPU e2e probe proves the shape gate.

E2E A/B (the real evidence, _conforming_budget_probe.cjs, npm run dev + node probe): BEFORE = committed e2e/baselines/authoritative-2026-06.json (7 capped styles at 400k: ArtDeco sliver=2107/maxAspect~1e9, DragonScales 2683/401,914, Crystalline 1972, Voronoi 1971, BasketWeave 1628, CelticTriquetra 1480, SpiralRidges 550). Run the minimum trio first (ArtDeco, DragonScales, SpiralRidges) at 400k + 499,998, then all 20 styles, plus 100k deep-refusal rows on ArtDeco+DragonScales. One diagnoseBudget call per row = one build; all instruments measure the SAME delivered mesh by construction. AFTER committed as e2e/baselines/budget-honesty-2026-06.json with the meshoptimizer version embedded. FIDELITY SPOT-CHECK (promoted from risk note to plan, since resultErrorMm is quadric-approximate, not Hausdorff): re-run the existing fidelity measure/crest instruments (measure(), diagnoseCrestQuality, __pfReferenceDenseRes path) on at least ONE 'applied' export and ONE capSaturated style so the STACKED chord error (capScale x sag + decimation error) is measured once before any fidelity statement is banked. NO-OP TRIPWIRE: all 20 styles at the production 'high' default budget â€” dec=not-needed in [CONFORMING-FULL] and mesh hashes match mesh-hashes-default-2026-06.json byte-identically. Finally detect_changes({scope:'compare', base_ref:'apply/streamlit-fix'}) before commit.
measurementGate:
Three enforced layers, A/B against committed artifacts (Stage-0 probe discipline); the gate is defined so an always-refusing implementation CANNOT pass (de-vacuated) and a fidelity claim cannot be banked unmeasured.

LAYER 1 â€” UNIT: the 11-test decimateConforming suite green, including the mock-level units pin (ErrorAbsolute + absolute-mm + stride=3 asserted on the captured simplify call â€” RED on HEAD), exact-subset, de-vacuated hard-assert apply, anisotropic shape gate, refusal, ladder guards, WASM-mock refusal, delta-gate-on-defective-input, and determinism. Whole existing suite (318+) green. Unit green is explicitly declared NON-EVIDENCE for the fix itself.

LAYER 2 â€” A/B PROBE (real WebGPU, default dims, the path that produced the finding): for EVERY row (20 styles x {400,000; 499,998} + ArtDeco/DragonScales x 100,000), the DELIVERED mesh must read sliver=0 AND maxAspect3D<100 AND bnd=0 AND orient=0 AND nonMan=0 (sole pre-existing carve-out: VoronoiCells nonMan<=2, tracked separately and coherent with the delta gate) AND featDrop=0 measured on the delivered (post-decision) vertex set from the SAME single build â€” AND exactly one of: (a) deliveredTriangles <= budget with decimation in {not-needed, applied} and decimationErrorMm within the report's own seed-to-ceiling bound, or (b) decimation='refused' with deliveredTriangles===builtTriangles and a non-empty machine-readable refusalReason. PRE-REGISTERED ACCEPTANCE ROWS (the de-vacuation teeth): at the Draft floor 499,998, ArtDeco (574,620 natural, -13%) and DragonScales (840,890, -41%) MUST be 'applied' and clean â€” a refusal on either FAILS the gate and escalates a named decision to the user (simplifyWithAttributes vertex_lock on feature-line vertices, or meshoptimizer 1.1.x + full re-baseline, or accept a refusal-dominant Draft tier) rather than silently passing. The probe also reports the accept/refuse split across all rows so the 'budget feature works vs is dead' question is answered with numbers. FIDELITY SPOT-CHECK (mandatory, in-gate): crest/sag instruments re-measured on >=1 applied export and >=1 capSaturated style â€” the stacked cap+decimation chord error is MEASURED once, never inferred from quadric resultErrorMm. AFTER matrix committed as e2e/baselines/budget-honesty-2026-06.json with meshoptimizer version embedded.

LAYER 3 â€” NO-OP INVARIANCE: all 20 styles at the production default ('high', ~4M budget, default dims) â€” the decimation branch must not fire (dec=not-needed) and mesh hashes are byte-identical to mesh-hashes-default-2026-06.json, proving the entire change is inert on the shipped default path.

PROCESS GATE (CLAUDE.md, non-negotiable): impact({target:'decimateConforming'}), impact({target:'summarizeConformingValidation'}), impact({target:'searchBudgetScale'}) reported to the user BEFORE editing (HIGH risk expected â€” export path); detect_changes({scope:'compare', base_ref:'apply/streamlit-fix'}) clean-scoped BEFORE commit.
productDecisions: FILE-SIZE CONTRACT (the headline sign-off): the dialog's MB slider becomes a REQUEST, not a promise. When the budget is unreachable without quality damage, the export SUCCEEDS with the natural watertight mesh and an honest notice â€” e.g. ArtDeco at a 25MB ask delivers ~574,620 tris (~29MB binary STL), DragonScales ~840,890 (~42MB); the default 3MF format compresses several-fold smaller. Today that exact scenario HARD-THROWS ('Export validation failed: N sliver triangle(s)'), so succeed-honestly-oversize is a strict upgrade â€” but Patryk must approve (a) the succeed-with-notice contract itself, and (b) whether the pre-download notice is passive (current blueprint) or a blocking confirm dialog.

DECIMATION FIDELITY CEILING = 0.2mm: an accepted decimation may spend up to 0.2mm chord error (~one FDM layer height), which is 4x the 'high' profile's 0.05mm sag. Sign off on 0.2mm or pick a lower ceiling (dev lever __pfDecimateErrCeilMm exists either way). Related: when the pre-triangulation cap coarsens (capScale>1), the report exposes effectiveMaxSagMm = capScale x sag and UI wording drops the 'full detail' claim â€” approve that copy.

PRE-REGISTERED DRAFT EXPECTATION AND ITS ESCALATION: the gate REQUIRES ArtDeco + DragonScales to be 'applied' (not refused) at the Draft floor 499,998. If physics refuses, the gate fails and Patryk chooses the escalation: simplifyWithAttributes vertex_lock on feature-line vertices (bigger change, same library), meshoptimizer 1.1.x Regularize improvements (requires full baseline re-measurement), or formally accepting a refusal-dominant Draft tier (Draft becomes 'best effort, often natural size').

BEHAVIORAL CONTRACT CHANGE FOR EXISTING FLOWS: low-budget conforming exports stop throwing and start succeeding with a larger file plus a warning. Any test, harness assertion, or UI flow that expects the 'Export validation failed' throw at small budgets must be updated in the same change â€” approve hunting those down as in-scope.

DEPENDENCY PIN: meshoptimizer pinned exactly to 1.0.1 (caret removed). Any future upgrade is a deliberate event that invalidates and re-baselines budget-honesty-2026-06.json (the version is recorded inside the artifact for detectability). Approve the pin.

BASELINE CHURN: authoritative-2026-06.json's 400k rows for the 7 capped styles flip from sliver>0 to refused-or-applied-clean; the b-sweep's sliver columns at capped budgets are already void per stage0-residuals.md. Approve re-baselining both alongside the new budget-honesty artifact (mesh-hashes-default-2026-06.json stays the unchanged tripwire).

rejected.idea: simplifyWithUpdate / simplifySloppy (meshopt variants that reposition vertices)
rejected.why: Violates the hard constraint outright: decimation must REMOVE vertices, never MOVE them â€” positions are exact analytic surface points. Plain simplify re-indexes over the untouched vertex array; the exact-subset unit test pins this forever.

Deletes whole disconnected components â€” a featureDrop and silent-geometry-loss hazard on styles with small raised elements; no measurement exists that would catch a pruned island except featDrop, which is vertex-proximity only.

rejected.idea: Post-hoc sliver deletion or any repair pass on the decimated mesh
rejected.why: Deleting detected slivers creates boundary edges (bnd>0), destroying watertightness-by-construction; repair passes are categorically banned by the task constraints and were the proven defect source the conforming rebuild eliminated.

rejected.idea: Raising MAX_BUDGET_SCALE above 4 so the cap alone always reaches budget
rejected.why: The cap's own documented rationale (ConformingWall.ts:176-183): coarsening multiplies the sag target, and a runaway scale bulldozes genuine sag-required detail. The scale-independent floors (crease lattice, feature refinement, boundary pinning) also make it ineffective on exactly the feature-dense styles that blow the budget.

rejected.idea: Trusting LockBorder / meshopt's internal topology handling for watertightness
rejected.why: LockBorder is provably inert on the closed pot (bnd=0 â€” nothing to lock) and upstream documents no shape/topology guarantee (issues #346/#477; 75-degree flip heuristic). Kept as free defense, never relied on â€” the per-candidate gate VERIFIES bnd/nonMan/orient/sliver/fold instead.

rejected.idea: Keeping relative error units and just lowering the 0.01 factor
rejected.why: Leaves the units semantics fragile and untestable â€” the exact bug class regresses with one constant edit. 'ErrorAbsolute' + a REQUIRED mm option (no default) + the mock-level units pin makes the regression structurally impossible, and ties the bound to the profile's physical sag.

rejected.idea: Pure Angle-B single pass at errorAbsMm = epsPosMm only (no ladder)
rejected.why: Decimating a sag-tight mesh below natural within 1x sag will usually refuse â€” the 'applied' branch becomes near-dead code, the gate goes vacuous (its own critique's words), and the Draft budget feature is dead on arrival. The bounded ladder (sag -> 0.2mm, max ~6 deterministic attempts) plus pre-registered ArtDeco/DragonScales acceptance rows keeps refusal honest where real and the feature alive where physics allows.

Silently trades unbounded fidelity for size â€” the opposite of the honesty contract â€” and breaks determinism and time-boundedness. The ladder is fixed, finite, restart-from-original, and ceilinged at a physically meaningful 0.2mm.

rejected.idea: Absolute-zero quality thresholds in the internal gate (refuse if candidate has ANY defect)
rejected.why: Critique-B fatal #3: pre-defective inputs (Voronoi nm=2 carve-out, extreme-dims naturals) could never take the applied branch, and 'quality-gate-failed' would misattribute pre-existing damage to decimation, corrupting the refusal-rate measurement that drives the escalation decision. Delta gating (no NEW defects) with inputDefects recorded fixes both.

rejected.idea: Re-running measureFeatureResolution post-acceptance via a report-carried index buffer indexed against asm.vertices at the call site
rejected.why: Critique-A's fatal flaw as originally sketched: dec.mesh.indices are compacted/renumbered and CANNOT index the asm.vertices stash; a separate post-hoc re-measure also duplicates work. The closure-stash inside validateCandidate measures the accepted candidate on RAW pre-compaction ids exactly once and becomes the shipped featDrop readout â€” acceptedIndices is still carried in the report for debuggability only.

rejected.idea: Inferring refusal in ExportPanel from meshData.triangleCount > tris
rejected.why: Misattributes the cause on the legacy path, wasm-unavailable, and simplify-threw cases ('quality loss' message when the truth is 'decimator unavailable'). The hook's synchronously-set lastBudgetReport carries the actual verdict and reason.

rejected.idea: Fixing the legacy meshDecimator.ts units + stride=12 bugs in this change
rejected.why: Real and shipping (the legacy GPU-grid 2M decimation has been geometrically wrong all along) but a separate blast radius with its own measurement; copying constants in either direction between the two decimators is exactly how the stride bug would propagate. Spawned as its own task (task_69e02801) with a standalone TDD prompt.

rejected.idea: Reviving profile maxTriangleBudget as a second budget source
rejected.why: Dead lever â€” the hook always passes targetTriangles from the dialog's MB conversion. Two budget sources reintroduce the ambiguity that made the current 400k probes surprising; the single source of truth stays the dialog request, with the profile contributing only the fidelity seed.

rejected.idea: refineCellInterior-style local remeshing of damaged regions instead of refusing
rejected.why: Already RULED OUT in the crest-serration work (affine off-center back-map is harmful on the curved surface), requires vertex creation/movement off the analytic surface, and is a repair pass by another name.
